notes: All 11 existing active specs cover the major subsystems in the import scope (agents, engine, db, api, world, rng, cli, dashboard, social, tribal-council, random-events).
src/utils/cost-tracker.ts is the only module in the import scope not covered by an existing spec; it has explicit pricing constants and budget thresholds.
No benchmark scripts, performance tests, or documented latency/throughput budgets were found — no benchmark sidecars drafted.
Vitest is configured in package.json but zero test files exist in the repository.
src/config.ts (22 lines, 3 exports) is implicitly covered by backend.engine and backend.cli specs.

uncertainties.detail: The cost tracker returns a withinBudget flag but source does not show the engine acting on a false value — unclear if per-tick overspend actually halts or throttles processing.
uncertainties.question: Does the engine currently enforce the per-tick budget cap, or is the flag informational only?

uncertainties.detail: Pricing constants are hard-coded; it is unclear if this is intentional or a placeholder for future configuration.
uncertainties.question: Should model pricing be configurable via environment variables or config file?
